description_long: This is a large, cylindrical metal barrel with a dark, weathered red finish and visible signs of rust and wear, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The barrel features two prominent horizontal reinforcement bands encircling its body, which are slightly raised and darker in color, providing structural integrity. The top surface is flat with a small, round, black bung or cap off-center, likely used for filling or emptying the contents. The surface texture is rough and matte, with patches of discoloration and grime, indicating it has been used for industrial or storage purposes. There are faint, barely visible markings or stenciled symbols on the side, possibly for identification or hazard warning, but no clear text is discernible. The overall appearance suggests it is a standard 55-gallon drum commonly found in urban, industrial, or construction environments.
description: A large, weathered red metal barrel with reinforcement bands and a small black cap on top, showing signs of rust and industrial use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical shape, two reinforcement bands, and a flat, rusted top with the cap not visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, this view reveals the small black cap on the top edge and faint side markings.
description_view_2: Another side view with the cap centered on top, showing the same weathered texture and reinforcement bands.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the cap visible on the left edge of the top, highlighting the rough, mottled surface.
